problem_summary: The repository lacks fundamental developer onboarding documentation (no README.md), has no issue/PR templates or commit message conventions for traceability, and its build script is a placeholder that performs no actual compilation or bundling. These gaps make it difficult for AI agents or new contributors to understand, set up, or contribute to the project in a structured way.

items.description: Create a comprehensive README.md with project introduction, feature list (math and string utility functions), tech stack (Node.js, Jest, ESLint, Prettier), prerequisites (Node.js >=18), installation steps (npm install), available scripts (npm test, npm run lint, npm run format), and a reference to .env.example for environment setup.
items.expected_benefit: Enables AI agents and new developers to immediately understand the project purpose, set up a local environment, and run available scripts without guessing or reading package.json manually.
items.implementation_risk: Minimal risk; purely additive documentation. Must be kept in sync with package.json scripts if they change.

items.description: Add local setup instructions in README.md covering: required Node.js version, how to install dependencies, how to configure environment variables from .env.example, how to run tests and linting, and how to use the pre-commit hooks via Husky.
items.affected_areas: README.md (same file, dedicated 'Getting Started' or 'Local Development' section)
items.expected_benefit: Provides a single source of truth for local setup, reducing onboarding friction and enabling AI agents to bootstrap the project autonomously.
items.implementation_risk: Very low; documentation-only change. Should reference .env.example accurately.

items.description: Add GitHub issue templates (.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.md and .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.md), a pull request template (.github/pull_request_template.md), and configure commitlint with the @commitlint/config-conventional preset plus a commit-msg Husky hook to enforce conventional commit messages.
items.expected_benefit: Establishes structured issue reporting, consistent PR descriptions, and machine-parseable commit messages, enabling automated changelogs and giving AI agents reliable traceability between issues, commits, and pull requests.
items.implementation_risk: Medium risk; adding commitlint may reject existing team members' commits if they are not following conventional commit format. Requires team communication and possibly a grace period.

items.description: Replace the placeholder 'build' script (currently 'echo "No build configured"') with either a real build step (e.g., using a bundler like esbuild or simply copying/validating source files) or, if no compilation is needed, remove the misleading build script and add a 'start' script (e.g., 'node src/index.js') so the project has a clear entry-point command.
items.affected_areas: package.json (scripts.build, scripts.start), potentially a new build configuration file (e.g., esbuild.config.js) if bundling is chosen
items.expected_benefit: Gives AI agents and CI pipelines a reliable build command to invoke, and provides a start script for running the project. Eliminates confusion from a no-op build script.
items.implementation_risk: Low-to-medium; if a bundler is added, it introduces a new dependency and configuration. A simpler approach (just adding a start script and removing the fake build) carries minimal risk.
